human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for housing operations execution, HUD-assisted housing and voucher specificity, resident-protection and inspection boundaries, functionality coverage, research summary, and runtime-specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest enforces housing operations execution boundaries for waitlist, tenant selection, certification, EIV, Form 50058, Form 50059, HCV RFTA, HAP, inspection, work-order, notice, resident protection, grievance intake, and completion evidence.
Authoritative sources cover named-industry operating guidance, workflow references tied to the lane, and evidence or control standards rather than only one research dimension.
Functionality map covers all required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.

Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, required source classes, required scenario families, and non-blocking ambiguity handling.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.

Scenario coverage includes normal housing operations, missing authoritative prerequisites, incomplete mid-process artifacts, rework escalation, unsupported completion, housing-specific prerequisite gaps, boundary overgeneralization, voucher finance routing, VAWA and fair housing escalation, terminology handling, conflicting policies, and tenant adaptation.

notes: validated conditions met for recommendation-only housing operations specialist.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance and commit history.
